data.title: Tether Mints Additional $1 Billion USDT on Ethereum Network
data.description: Tether has minted another $1 billion USDT on the Ethereum network. According to ChainCatcher, this brings the total amount minted in the past five days to $3 billion.

data.title: Cognition AI in Early Talks for New Funding Round to Double Valuation
data.description:
Cognition AI is reportedly in the early stages of negotiations for a new funding round, according to ChainCatcher. The company aims to capitalize on the growing demand for generative artificial intelligence technology. Sources indicate that this round of financing could more than double the company's valuation to $25 billion.

One insider revealed that the AI programming startup is targeting to raise several hundred million dollars or more. The discussions are ongoing, and the terms may change.

data.title: AI TRENDS | OpenAI and NVIDIA Collaborate to Enhance Employee Productivity
data.description:
On April 24, OpenAI founder Sam Altman shared an email from NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang on social media. According to Jin10, Huang's email, addressed to all NVIDIA employees, encouraged the use of OpenAI's Codex programming tool. He urged the company's 10,000 employees across various departments, including engineering, product, legal, marketing, finance, sales, and human resources, to actively utilize this tool and its intelligent agents, describing them as 'teammates' and 'superpowers' for team collaboration.

Huang revealed that NVIDIA and OpenAI have collaborated within Endeavor to establish Codex Labs, providing learning support and hosting a series of online seminars aimed at enhancing employee efficiency.

data.title: Whales added 28.3 billion ASTEROID tokens in the past 9 hours, bringing their total holdings to 6.66 billion tokens.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, the whale 0x2D2D spent 368.23 ETH (approximately $857,000) to buy 28.3 billion ASTEROIDs in the past 9 hours. This address now holds a total of 6.66 billion ASTEROIDs, worth approximately $1.99 million.

data.title: The US Solomon Islands (SOL) spot ETF saw a total net inflow of $7.3314 million in a single day.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, yesterday (April 23rd, Eastern Time), the total net inflow into SOL spot ETFs was $7.3314 million. The SOL spot ETF with the largest net inflow yesterday was the Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL), with a net inflow of $6.2012 million, bringing its total historical net inflow to $825 million. The second largest was the VanEck Solana ETF (VSOL), with a net inflow of $1.1302 million, also bringing its total historical net inflow to $1.1302 million. As of press time, the total net asset value of SOL spot ETFs is $874 million, with an SOL net asset value ratio of 1.77%, and a cumulative historical net inflow of $1.02 billion.

data.title: Mantle Community Considers Strategic Loan to Aave
data.description: The Mantle community is currently deliberating a governance proposal that involves a strategic loan of up to 30,000 ETH to assist Aave. According to NS3.AI, Mantle clarified that the initiative is a strategic loan rather than unconditional support. Meanwhile, Ethena has indicated that discussions regarding its own support are in progress.

data.title: OpenAI officially released GPT-5.5, significantly improving intelligence and efficiency.
data.description: According to official news, OpenAI has officially released GPT-5.5. This model represents a significant advancement in intelligent agent programming, computer use, and scientific research, achieving an accuracy of 82.7% in the Terminal-Bench 2.0 test. While maintaining the same latency as GPT-5.4, GPT-5.5 offers higher inference efficiency and consumes fewer tokens. Currently, GPT-5.5 is available to ChatGPT Plus, Pro, and enterprise users, and an API version will be launched soon. The pricing is $5 per 1 million input tokens and $30 per 1 million output tokens. In addition, GPT-5.5 Pro, designed for more challenging tasks, has also been released simultaneously.

data.title: Mantle plans to provide Aave with a loan of 30,000 ETH, and the confirmed bailout funds cover approximately 43,500 ETH.
data.description: According to information from the governance forum, Bybit's public blockchain Mantle plans to provide Aave with a loan of 30,000 ETH to address the bad debt risk caused by the recent security incident. According to crypto analyst EmberCN, the confirmed relief funds currently cover a shortfall of approximately 43,500 ETH.

data.title: Bitmine Staked Over 191,000 ETH in 12 Hours
data.description: Bitmine has staked a total of 191,952 ETH in the past 12 hours, according to ChainCatcher. This includes an additional 98,352 ETH staked in the last three hours. The total value of the staked ETH is approximately $446.9 million.

data.title: Ether.fi Enhances Security Measures for weETH Cross-Chain Bridge
data.description: Ether.fi announced on the X platform that the team has completed protocol-level security enhancements for the weETH cross-chain bridge across most deployment chains. According to PANews, no vulnerabilities have occurred, and potential risks have been proactively mitigated, with all security-related bridge parameters now fully controlled by ether.fi. Specific changes include locking the LayerZero send/receive message library, locking the DVN set and raising the threshold to 4/4, and tightening the pair rate limit on ether.fi's bridge contracts. Following these changes, LayerZero's multi-signature cannot modify the weETH bridge configuration on-chain, and each inbound message requires verification by all four DVNs. Ether.fi's next step is to evaluate the introduction of a second independent cross-chain stack outside of LayerZero and conduct a systematic risk assessment of the chains where weETH is located.

data.title: Republican leaders are demanding that Lutnick halt the political involvement of the encrypted PAC in Texas.
data.description: Senior Republican officials recently called Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick, urging him to intervene in the Texas spending plans of the Fellowship PAC, a crypto political action committee backed by his former company Cantor Fitzgerald. The PAC had previously disclosed in an FEC filing that it planned to spend $1.75 million to support Ken Paxton, raising concerns within the party about its involvement in the sensitive primaries. Sources say the PAC ultimately did not run the ads, and Republicans subsequently stated that concerns had subsided. The Fellowship PAC, which is affiliated with Tether, previously stated its intention to raise $100 million for the 2026 election and has so far raised approximately $11 million. (Axios)

data.title: ether.fi plans to invest 5,000 ETH in the rsETH bailout.
data.description: The EtherFi Foundation has proposed injecting 5,000 ETH into a dedicated rsETH relief pool to fill collateral gaps and prevent bad debts from spreading within the DeFi system. This proposal is part of the "DeFi United" joint relief effort, aimed at restoring rsETH collateral support and stabilizing the lending market.

data.title: Arbitrum Security Council Freezes Over 30,000 ETH After KelpDAO Attack
data.description:
On April 24, following the KelpDAO attack, the Arbitrum Security Council intervened to freeze more than 30,000 ETH. According to BlockBeats, this action successfully prevented the transfer of some stolen funds but sparked intense debate over the boundaries of decentralization.

The operation was carried out by a 12-member security council elected by token holders, who used special permissions to transfer the attacker's funds to an 'ownerless wallet,' effectively freezing them. Supporters argue that this move bought crucial time for the industry, preventing further laundering of the funds and serving as a necessary 'security-first' mechanism.

Critics, however, contend that this case demonstrates that even in so-called decentralized networks, a few individuals can intervene and alter on-chain outcomes at critical moments. This challenges the core principle of 'code is law' and raises concerns about potential future misuse.

Arbitrum responded by stating that the mechanism was designed to be transparent and community-authorized, serving as a 'last line of defense in extreme situations.' They emphasized that it reflects a balance between security and decentralization, rather than a rejection of the latter.

data.title: Aave founder Stani announced a personal donation of 5,000 ETH to address current issues.
data.description: Aave founder Stani posted on X: "Aave is my life's work, and we are working day and night to find the best solutions for our users. I have personally donated 5,000 ETH to DeFi United, and we will continue to work with our partners to fulfill more commitments. I am committed to resolving the current issues as soon as possible and restoring the market environment to normal."

data.title: The rsETH incident created a shortfall of approximately 68,900 ETH, and various parties have donated 13,500 ETH.
data.description: According to crypto analyst EmberCN, the rsETH incident has created a shortfall of approximately 68,900 ETH, equivalent to about $160 million. This shortfall was formed by the attackers staking rsETH to borrow 99,600 ETH, minus the 30,700 ETH recovered by Arbitrum. The relevant ETH has now been converted to BTC, and the incident has entered the recovery phase. Aave has coordinated the establishment of "DeFi United" for fund recovery, and various parties have provided approximately 13,500 ETH in support, including 2,500 stETH from Lido, 5,000 ETH from ether.fi, 5,000 ETH from Aave founder Stani Kulechov, and 1,000 ETH from the Golem Foundation. Other institutions have not disclosed their specific amounts.

data.title: U.S.-Iran Tensions Escalate Amid Strategic Military Movements
data.description:
On April 24, tensions between the United States and Iran have intensified, with Iran accusing the U.S. and Israel of being the root causes of regional instability. According to BlockBeats, Iran has prepared a list of counterattack targets and is expanding its military arsenal, while its leadership has denied any internal divisions. The U.S. has advised its citizens to leave Iran, and U.S. President Donald Trump reiterated that nuclear weapons will not be used, asserting that the U.S. military has completed its 'strongest' deployment in the Middle East. He warned of potential expanded strikes if the ceasefire fails.

Israel has heightened its military readiness, purchasing $200 million in aviation munitions, with its defense minister stating readiness to resume operations against Iran. Meanwhile, clashes continue in southern Lebanon, with Hezbollah engaging in ongoing skirmishes with Israeli forces.

The confrontation over the Strait of Hormuz has intensified, with the U.S. intercepting Iranian oil tankers. Trump has threatened to sink mine-laying vessels and even 'completely block' the strait. Iran has begun charging transit fees, marking a new phase in the standoff.

Ceasefire negotiations face increasing challenges, with the U.S. maintaining that the ceasefire remains effective, while Iran suggests potential short-term breakthroughs but insists that negotiations will not proceed unless the U.S. concedes. The focus of talks has shifted from nuclear issues to a comprehensive ceasefire.

Key points to watch include whether the strait will be blocked and if the ceasefire will collapse, both of which could directly impact oil prices and global market risk preferences.

data.title: Japan's March Corporate Service Price Index Rises 3.1%
data.description: Japan's Corporate Service Price Index (CSPI) for March showed an annual increase of 3.1%, surpassing the expected 3.00% and higher than the previous value of 2.70%. According to Jin10, this data reflects the ongoing trends in Japan's service sector pricing.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump Warns of Tariffs on UK Over Digital Tax
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump, in an interview with The Daily Telegraph, stated that if the United Kingdom does not revoke its digital tax on American tech companies, he will impose significant tariffs on the UK. According to Jin10, the UK Treasury previously disclosed that it had collected millions of pounds in digital services tax last year. Trump expressed his disapproval of the UK's actions against American companies, saying, "I don't like what they are doing to our companies." He added, "We have been monitoring this situation, and we can easily address it by imposing substantial tariffs on the UK. They better be careful. If they do not cancel this tax, we are very likely to impose significant tariffs on the UK." Trump believes that the tax unfairly targets American tech giants such as Apple, Google, and Meta.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Announces Successful Meeting with Israeli and Lebanese Representatives
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump announced on April 24 that a meeting took place at the White House with senior representatives from Israel and Lebanon. According to BlockBeats, the discussions were highly successful, with the U.S. pledging to assist Lebanon in safeguarding itself against threats from Hezbollah.

The ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon has been extended for three weeks. President Trump expressed his anticipation of welcoming Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Lebanese President Michel Aoun in the near future.

data.title: STABLE Experiences 14.6% Surge, Approaches Key Resistance Level
data.description: STABLE saw a significant increase of 14.6% on April 23, reaching a value of $0.02999. According to NS3.AI, this rise brought the token close to the $0.03059 Fibonacci resistance, which has previously limited upward movements. Chart analysis suggests that if STABLE surpasses this resistance, it could target levels between $0.03566 and $0.03673. However, if the breakout does not succeed, the crucial support level to watch is $0.02649.
